author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter X. 22–42.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As I have already charged you, beloved, you ought steadfastly to bear in mind that Saint John the evangelist would not have us be always nourished with milk, but fed with solid food.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Still, whoever is hardly able as yet to partake of the solid food of God’s word, let him find nourishment in the milk of faith; and the word which he cannot understand, let him not hesitate to believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For faith is the deserving: understanding, the reward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the very labor of intent application the eye of our mind struggles to get rid of the foul films of human mists, and be cleared up to the word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Labor, then, will not be declined if love is present; for you know that he who loves his labor is insensible to its pain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no labor is grievous to those who love it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If cupidity on the part of the avaricious endures so great toils, what in our case will not love endure?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the Gospel: “And it was at Jerusalem the Encœnia.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Encoenia was the festival of the dedication of the temple.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in Greek kainos means new; and whenever there was some new dedication, it was called Encœnia.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now this word is come into common use; if one puts on a new coat, he is said “encœniare” (to renovate, or to hold an encœnia).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Jews celebrated in a solemn manner the day on which the temple was dedicated; and it was the very feast day when the Lord spake what has just been read.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It was winter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon’s porch.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then came the Jews round about Him, and said unto Him, How long dost thou keep our mind in suspense?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were not desiring the truth, but preparing a calumny.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It was winter,” and they were chill; because they were slow to approach that divine fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to approach is to believe: he who believes, approaches; who denies, retires.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul is not moved by the feet, but by the affections.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They had become icy cold to the sweetness of loving Him, and they burned with the desire of doing Him an injury.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were far away, while there beside Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not with them a nearer approach in believing, but the pressure of persecution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They sought to hear the Lord saying, I am Christ; and probably enough they only thought of the Christ in a human way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The prophets preached Christ; but the Godhead of Christ asserted in the prophets and in the gospel itself is not perceived even by heretics; and how much less by Jews, so long as the vail is upon their heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, in a certain place, the Lord Jesus, knowing that their views of the Christ were cast in a human mould, not in the Divine, taking His stand on the human ground, and not on that where along with the assumption of humanity He also continued Divine, He said to them, “What think ye of Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whose Son is He?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Following their own opinion, they replied, “Of David.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so they had read, and this only they retained; because while they read of His divinity, they did not understand it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord, to pin them down to some inquiry touching the divinity of Him whose apparent weakness they despised, answered them: “How, then, doth David in spirit call Him Lord, saying, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on my right hand, till I put Thine enemies under Thy feet?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If David, then, in spirit call Him Lord, how is He his son?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not deny, but questioned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no one think, on hearing this, that the Lord Jesus denied that He was the Son of David.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had Christ the Lord given any such denial, He would not have enlightened the blind who so addressed Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as He was passing by one day, two blind men, who were sitting by the wayside, cried out, “Have mercy upon us, thou Son of David.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And on hearing these words He had mercy on them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He stood still, healed, enlightened them; for He owned the name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul also says, “Who was made of the seed of David according to the flesh;” and in his Epistle to Timothy, “Remember that Jesus Christ was raised from the dead, [He that is] of the seed of David, according to my gospel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Virgin Mary drew her origin, and hence our Lord also, from the seed of David.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews made this inquiry of Christ, chiefly in order that, should He say, I am Christ, they might, in accordance with the only sense they attached to such a name, that He was of the seed of David, calumniate Him with aiming at the kingly power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is more than this in His answer to them: they wished to calumniate Him with claiming to be the Son of David.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He replied that He was the Son of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen: “Jesus answered them, I tell you, and ye believe not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me: but ye believe not; because ye are not of my sheep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye have already learned above (in Lecture XLV.) who the sheep are: be ye sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are sheep through believing, sheep in following the Shepherd, sheep in not despising their Redeemer, sheep in entering by the door, sheep in going out and finding pasture, sheep in the enjoyment of eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did He mean, then, in saying to them, “Ye are not of my sheep”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He saw them predestined to everlasting destruction, not won to eternal life by the price of His own blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me: and I give unto them eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the pasture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you recollect, He had said before, “And he shall go in and out, and find pasture.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have entered by believing—we go out at death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as we have entered by the door of faith, so, as believers, we quit the body; for it is in going out by that same door that we are able to find pasture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The good pasture is called eternal life; there no blade withereth—all is green and flourishing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a plant commonly said to be ever-living; there only is it found to live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I will give,” He says, “unto them,” unto my sheep, “eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye are on the search for calumnies, just because your only thoughts are of the life that is present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And they shall never perish:” you may hear the undertone, as if He had said to them, Ye shall perish for ever, because ye are not of my sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “No one shall pluck them out of my hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give still greater heed to this: “That which my Father gave me is greater than all.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can the wolf do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can the thief and the robber?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They destroy none but those predestined to destruction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But of those sheep of which the apostle says, “The Lord knoweth them that are His;” and “Whom He did foreknow, them He also did predestinate; and whom He did predestinate, them He also called; and whom He called, them He also justified; and whom He justified, them He also glorified;”—there is none of such sheep as these that the wolf seizes, or the thief steals, or the robber slays.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, who knows what He gave for them, is sure of their number.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it is this that He says: “No one shall pluck them out of my hand;” and in reference also to the Father, “That which my Father gave me is greater than all.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did the Father give to the Son that was greater than all?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To be His own only-begotten Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, means “gave”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was He to whom He gave previously existent, or gave He in the act of begetting?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He previously existed to whom He gave the gift of Sonship, there was a time when He was, and was not the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far be it from us to suppose that the Lord Christ ever was, and yet was not the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of us such a thing may be said: there was a time when we were the sons of men, but were not the sons of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are made the sons of God by grace, but He by nature, for such was He born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet not so, as that one may say, He did not exist till He was born; for He, who was coeternal with the Father, was never unborn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him who is wise understand: and whoever understands not, let him believe and be nourished, and he will come to understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word of God was always with the Father, and always the Word; and because the Word, therefore the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So then, always the Son, and always equal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not by growth but by birth that He is equal, who was always born, the Son of the Father, God of God, coeternal of the Eternal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Father is not God of the Son: the Son is God of the Father; therefore in begetting the Son, the Father “gave” Him to be God, in begetting He gave Him to be coeternal with Himself, in begetting He gave Him to be His equal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is that which is greater than all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is the Son the life, and the possessor of life?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What He has, He is: as for thee, thou art one thing, thou hast another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For example, thou hast wisdom, but art thou wisdom itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, because thou thyself art not that which thou hast, shouldst thou lose what thou hast, thou returnest to the state of no longer having it: and sometimes thou re-acquirest, sometimes thou losest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As our eye has no light inherently in itself, it opens, and admits it; it shuts, and loses it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not thus that the Son of God is God—not thus that He is the Word of the Father; and not thus is He the Word, that passes away with the sound, but that which abides in its birth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In such a way hath He wisdom that He is Himself wisdom, and maketh men wise: and life, that He is Himself the life, and maketh others alive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is that which is greater than all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The evangelist John himself looked to heaven and earth when wishing to speak of the Son of God; he looked, and rose above them all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He thought on the thousands of angelic armies above the heavens; he thought, and, like the eagle soaring beyond the clouds, his mind overpassed the whole creation: he rose beyond all that was great, and arrived at that which was greater than all; and said, “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because He, of whom is the Word, is not of the Word, and the Word is of Him, whose Word He is; therefore He says, “That which the Father gave me,” namely, to be His Word, His only-begotten Son, the brightness of His light, “is greater than all.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “No one,” He says, “plucketh my sheep out of my hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No one can pluck them out of my Father’s hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Out of my hand,” and “out of my Father’s hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, “No one plucketh them out of my hand,” and “No one plucketh them out of my Father’s hand”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have the Father and Son one hand, or is the Son Himself, shall we say, the hand of His Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If by hand we are to understand power, the power of Father and Son is one; for their Godhead is one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we mean hand in the way spoken of by the prophet, “And to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?” the Father’s hand is the Son Himself, which is not to be so understood as if God had the human form, and, as it were, bodily members: but that all things were made by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For men also are in the habit of calling other men their hands, by whom they get done what they wish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And sometimes also the very work done by a man’s hand is called his hand; as one is said to recognize his hand when he recognizes what he has written.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, then, there are many ways of speaking of the hand of a man, who literally has a hand among the members of his body; how much rather must there be more than one way of understanding it, when we read of the hand of God, who has no bodily form?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in this way it is better here, by the hand of the Father and Son, to understand the power of the Father and the Son; lest, in taking here the hand of the Father as spoken of the Son, some carnal thought also about the Son Himself should set us looking for the Son as somehow to be similarly regarded as the hand of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “no one plucketh them out of my Father’s hand;” that is, no one plucketh them from me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that there may be no more room for hesitation, hear what follows: “I and my Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Up to this point the Jews were able to bear Him; they heard, “I and my Father are one,” and they bore it no longer; and hardened in their own way, they had recourse to stones.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They took up stones to stone Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, because He suffered not what He was unwilling to suffer, and only suffered what He was pleased to suffer, still addresses them while desiring to stone Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Jews took up stones to stone Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I showed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they answered, For a good work we stone thee not, but for blasphemy, and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such was their reply to His words, “I and my Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see here that the Jews understood what the Arians understand not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they were angry on this account, that they felt it could not be said, “I and my Father are one,” save where there was equality of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But see what answer the Lord gave to their dull apprehension.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saw that they could not bear the brilliance of the truth, and He tempered it with words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Is it not written in your law,” that is, as given to you, “that I said, Ye are gods?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord called all the Scriptures generally, the law: although elsewhere He speaks more definitely of the law, distinguishing it from the prophets; as it is said, “The law and the prophets were until John;” and “On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sometimes, however, He divided the same Scriptures into three parts, as where He saith, “All things must be fulfilled which were written in the law, and the prophets, and the psalms, concerning me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now He includes the psalms also under the name of the law, where it is written, “I said, Ye are gods.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He calleth them gods, to whom the word of God came, and the Scripture cannot be broken: say ye of Him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the word of God came to men, that they might be called gods, how can the very Word of God, who is with God, be otherwise than God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If by the word of God men become gods, if by fellowship they become gods, can He by whom they have fellowship not be God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If lights which are lit are gods, is the light which enlighteneth not God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If through being warmed in a way by saving fire they are constituted gods, is He who gives them the warmth other than God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou approachest the light and art enlightened, and numbered among the sons of God; if thou withdrawest from the light, thou fallest into obscurity, and art accounted in darkness; but that light approacheth not, because it never recedeth from itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the word of God maketh you gods, how can the Word of God be otherwise than God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore did the Father sanctify His Son, and send Him into the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some one may be saying: If the Father sanctified Him, was there then a time when He was not sanctified?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sanctified in the same way as He begat Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the act of begetting He gave Him the power to be holy, because He begat Him in holiness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if that which is sanctified was unholy before, how can we say to God the Father, “Hallowed be Thy name”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if I do, though ye will not believe me, believe the works; that ye may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son says not, “the Father is in me, and I in Him,” as men can say it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we think well, we are in God; and if we live well, God is in us: believers, by participating in His grace, and being illuminated by Himself, are in Him, and He in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But not so is it with the only-begotten Son: He is in the Father, and the Father in Him; as one who is equal is in him whose equal he is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, we can sometimes say, We are in God, and God is in us; but can we say, I and God are one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art in God, because God contains thee; God is in thee, because thou art become the temple of God: but because thou art in God, and God is in thee, canst thou say, He that seeth me seeth God; as the Only-begotten said, “He that hath seen me, hath seen the Father also;” and “I and the Father are one”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Recognize the prerogative of the Lord, and the privilege of the servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The prerogative of the Lord is equality with the Father: the privilege of the servant is fellowship with the Saviour.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore they sought to apprehend Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would they had apprehended by faith and understanding, not in wrath and murder!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For now, my brethren, when I speak thus, it is the weak one wishing to apprehend what is strong, the small what is great, the fragile what is solid; and it is we ourselves—both you who are of the same matter as I am, and I myself who speak to you—who all wish to apprehend Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is it to apprehend Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: [If] thou hast understood, thou hast apprehended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But not as did the Jews: thou hast apprehended in order to possess, they wished to apprehend in order to make away with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because this was the kind of apprehension they desired, what did He do to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He escaped out of their hands.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They failed to apprehend Him, because they lacked the hand of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word was made flesh; but it was no great task to the Word to rescue His own flesh from fleshy hands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To apprehend the Word in the mind, is the right apprehension of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And He went away again beyond Jordan, into the place where John at first baptized; and there He abode.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And many resorted unto Him, and said, John, indeed, did no miracle.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember what was said of John, that he was a light, and bore witness to the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, say these among themselves, “John did no miracle”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John, they say, signalized himself by no miracle; he did not put devils to flight, he drove away no fever, he enlightened not the blind, he raised not the dead, he fed not so many thousand men with five or seven loaves, he walked not upon the sea, he commanded not the winds and the waves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: None of these things did John, and in all he said he bore witness to this man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By lamp-light we may advance to the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here are those who apprehended in a different way from the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews wished to apprehend one who was departing from them, these apprehended one who remained with them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, what is it that follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And many believed on Him.”
